title.purpose: Page-level headers, e.g. 'Biblioteca', novel titles. Never a second typeface — weight and size only.

body.purpose: Default UI copy, forms, lists, buttons.

reading.purpose: Chapter/reader content only — larger, looser line-height than UI body for long-form comfort.

shadows.name: cover-rest
shadows.purpose: Default elevation for novel cover art in the library grid.

shadows.name: cover-lift
shadows.purpose: Hover/focus-visible state on a cover, paired with translateY(-3px).

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default hover/focus transitions (buttons, cover lift, menu button opacity).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Pill-shaped, Warm Ink fill, no border or shadow.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #1c1917; color: #fafaf9; border: none; border-radius: 999px; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.9375rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #292524; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2.5px solid #57544c; outline-offset: 2px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgba(87,84,76,0.18); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #f5f4f2; color: #141413; border: 1px solid rgba(20,20,19,0.14); border-radius: 999px; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.9375rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #e8e6e2; }

components.name: Novel Cover Card
components.description: The library's signature object: 2:3 cover art, flat-bordered frame at rest, real shadow + lift on hover.
components.html: Título de la Novela
components.css: .ds-cover-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; width: 160px; } .ds-cover-frame { aspect-ratio: 2/3; border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid rgba(20,20,19,0.14); background: #f5f4f2; box-shadow: 0 8px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); transition: transform 0.15s ease, box-shadow 0.15s ease; } .ds-cover-frame img { width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; display: block; } .ds-cover-card:hover .ds-cover-frame, .ds-cover-card:focus-within .ds-cover-frame { transform: translateY(-3px); box-shadow: 0 14px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.14); } .ds-cover-title { margin-top: 0.375rem; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.8125rem; line-height: 1.3; color: #141413; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Flat bordered field, neutral focus ring.
components.css: .ds-input { background: #ffffff; color: #141413; border: 1px solid rgba(20,20,19,0.14); border-radius: 12px; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; font-size: 1rem; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: rgba(20,20,19,0.48); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2.5px solid #57544c; outline-offset: 2px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgba(87,84,76,0.18); }

components.name: Top Navigation
components.description: Sticky, translucent, blurred top bar — recedes over scroll content.
components.css: .ds-topbar { position: sticky; top: 0; display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 56px; padding: 0.5rem 1.25rem; background: rgba(250,250,249,0.92); backdrop-filter: blur(12px); border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(20,20,19,0.14); } .ds-brand { font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.0625rem; color: #141413; }

components.name: Status Tag
components.css: .ds-tag { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 0.125rem 0.5rem; border-radius: 999px; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; } .ds-tag-success { background: color-mix(in oklab, #16a34a 18%, #e8e6e2); color: #141413; }

narrative.overview: Yara is built on the calm materiality of a physical bookshelf: warm stone and ink tones, no color for color's sake, no chrome that competes with the spine of the book. Per PRODUCT.md, "chrome recedes; the text and cover art lead" — the interface behaves like Readest or Calibre for library polish, and like Mihon/Tachiyomi for the "manage and consume" duality across desktop and mobile. Nothing here performs for attention; everything is furniture in a reading room. The palette is a single warm-neutral ramp (bone paper to warm ink) rather than a saturated brand hue, because the "brand" is the reading experience itself, not a marketing identity. Color is reserved almost entirely for semantic state (job status, danger actions) — never for decoration. This system explicitly rejects the dense "admin dashboard" look (data-tables-everywhere, SaaS metric cards) and any layout that treats mobile as a shrunk-down desktop view.
keyCharacteristics: Warm-stone monochrome palette; color appears only as semantic signal, never decoration.
Flat by default; the one deliberate exception is the novel cover, which gets real elevation because it's the object being handled, not the chrome around it.
Full light/dark/system theming via a single token layer, not a bolted-on dark mode.
Motion is short and functional (0.15–0.2s), never choreographed.

rules.name: The One Ramp Rule
rules.body: There is exactly one hue family (warm stone/graphite). If a screen needs a second color, it must be a semantic state color from the fixed set — never a new decorative hue.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Geist is the only typeface in the system. Emphasis comes from weight (400/600/700) and size, never from switching families or adding a display serif.

rules.name: The Flat-Except-The-Book Rule
rules.body: Every surface is flat and bordered at rest. The only thing allowed to lift off the page is a book cover, because that's the one object the user is meant to reach for.

dos: Do keep the entire UI on one hue family (warm stone/graphite); introduce color only via the fixed semantic set (info/success/warn/danger).
Do let the novel cover be the only element with real box-shadow and hover lift; every other surface stays flat with a 1px border.
Do size touch targets to at least 44px and treat mobile layouts as first-class, not shrunk desktop.
Do keep transitions short (0.15–0.2s) and purposeful — a hover lift, a drawer slide, a focus ring — never a choreographed entrance.
Do use the pill radius (999px) for buttons and tags, and the 12–20px scale for cards/surfaces.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion for every transition.
donts: Don't introduce a second decorative hue or gradient — this is explicitly a one-ramp system.
Don't build a dense "admin dashboard" look (data-tables-everywhere, SaaS metric cards).
Don't add drop shadows to generic cards, dialogs, or buttons — depth comes from tonal surface steps and borders, not shadow, except the novel cover.
Don't mix in a second typeface for "display" moments — Geist carries every role via weight and size.
Don't design mobile as an afterthought breakpoint; reading and library browsing must feel native-smooth on a phone.
Don't use side-stripe borders, gradient text, or glassmorphism as decoration — the one blur in the system (topbar/menu-button backdrop-filter) is functional legibility-over-scroll, not decoration.
